props.label: At a Glance · From the Tape Desk

bullets.text: Brent settled at $88.10 and WTI at $82.49 on Friday as Hormuz risk returned and the world’s supply buffers ran drained, while the Philadelphia semiconductor index fell about 20% from its peak even after TSMC’s record quarter and 77% earnings growth.
The week ahead keeps the Fed at 3.50–3.75% with no meeting until 28–29 July, the ECB decides on 23 July, the Bank of Japan meets 30–31 July, and the Bank of Korea has already hiked to 2.75%.
Gold near $4,011 finished the week down about 2.6%, the 10-year Treasury yield sat at 4.57%, and the dollar held near 100.76 in a pattern of supply-shock pricing.

items.what: World Cup final, 3 p.m. ET, MetLife
items.why: Security-incident, air-quality and FIFA-charge calls resolve at kickoff and after the match.
The deposit rate sits at 2.25% after June’s hike; the tone will frame Europe’s response to the energy shock.
After eight nights and fresh casualties, any halt would mark the first off-ramp since the truce collapsed.
A roughly $8.6bn chip IPO tests appetite for the AI-compute trade after a sharp selloff.
The FOMC holds at 3.50–3.75% with the war acting as an inflationary supply shock.
A settle at or above $90 would confirm a durable Hormuz risk premium.

props.meta: Calibrated calls · 2026-07-19 · Vesta, Sprockett, Tinkerton, Graves, Foreman · the Colosseum arc’s calls settle at tonight’s final; one prior call resolved this edition

open_calls.question: The Spain–Argentina final is played without a security incident causing reported injuries or arrests at MetLife
open_calls.detail: The final sits under an FBI-led operation its director called the biggest in the bureau’s history, with an FAA no-drone-zone and a protected head of state; the base rate for a major incident at such an event is low, though the target profile on this particular day is unusually high.

open_calls.question: Northeastern New Jersey records an air-quality index of 101 or higher during the final
open_calls.detail: Canadian wildfire haze lingered into final morning, but a drier, warmer afternoon is forecast; no official during-match reading exists yet and residual smoke remains possible.

open_calls.question: FIFA issues a formal disciplinary charge against the Argentine federation over the Malvinas banner before kickoff
open_calls.detail: FIFA has weighed proceedings and the comparable 2014 fine landed after that tournament; a formal charge before this evening’s kickoff is less likely than one after it, and a White House task-force official has backed the players.

open_calls.question: The United States and Iran announce a ceasefire or a formal pause in strikes
open_calls.detail: The campaign has run eight consecutive nights with fresh U.S. casualties and Iranian retaliation on Gulf bases, the interim truce collapsed last week, and Iran’s new leader is refusing negotiation while attacks continue; a pause within the week is less likely than continuation.

open_calls.question: Front-month Brent crude settles at or above $90 a barrel
open_calls.detail: Brent settled near $88 on Friday and traders warn the buffers that absorbed the first round are depleted; a settle above $90 needs a further leg, but a deeper Hormuz disruption would supply it.

open_calls.question: Federal authorities publish an after-action accounting of enforcement at tournament venues
open_calls.detail: The arc’s standing discriminator on the enforcement shadow: if a documented federal accounting appears by mid-August the shadow takes shape, and if it does not the null holds; agencies rarely publish venue-level enforcement data on that timeline.

items.what: Brent and WTI held their war premium into the weekend at $88.10 and $82.49, with the Strait of Hormuz throttled and refined-product margins at records.
The semiconductor index fell about 20% from its peak and the KOSPI more than 20% from its June record, an AI-trade de-rating even as TSMC, Micron and Nvidia guided higher.
Gold near $4,011 slipped 2.6% on the week while Treasury yields eased and the dollar held, a mixed safe-haven pattern consistent with supply-shock pricing.
